01_initial_request.target_name: Benchmark forecasting model for emergency ambulance services
01_initial_request.input_data: Time series data of daily ambulance dispatch calls from South West England, London, Yorkshire, and Welsh Ambulance Services
01_initial_request.user_intent: To select and externally validate a benchmark method for emergency ambulance services to forecast demand and aid workforce planning
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Mean absolute scaled error and 80% and 95% prediction interval coverage over an 84-day horizon

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Emergency ambulance services require accurate demand forecasting for workforce planning. Existing studies lack external validation and scale-independent accuracy measures. [Gap] No established benchmark exists for comparing forecasting methods across ambulance trusts. [Decision] To establish a robust benchmark, chose to use Python and R statistical libraries to prepare and analyze time series data from multiple ambulance services, expecting to obtain cleaned daily dispatch call counts for model development.

parameters.data_sources: South Western Ambulance Service NHS Foundation Trust (SWASFT), London Ambulance Service (LAS), Welsh Ambulance Service Trust (WAST), Yorkshire Ambulance Service (YAS)
parameters.observation_type: daily count of emergency calls resulting in ambulance dispatch
parameters.sub_regions_SWASFT: 7 regions (Devon, Cornwall, Dorset, Somerset, Gloucestershire, Wiltshire, BNSSG)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Training data prepared with no missing observations. Median daily calls: 2169 (IQR 2083-2269). Extreme observations on New Year's day with median 2783 calls. Slight upward trend from 2135 (2014) to 2257 (2018).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multiple forecasting methods exist but need systematic comparison against a baseline. [Gap] Need to establish which methods outperform simple baseline before selecting benchmark. [Decision] To screen candidate models, chose to use Seasonal Naive method as baseline with rolling forecast origin cross-validation, expecting to identify methods that provide value over simplest approach.

parameters.method: uses most recent observation for same day of week carried forward

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Naive method achieved MASE of 0.94 (0.35) over 7 days. Over 84-day horizon MASE increased to 1.34 (30% increase). By 365 days MASE reached 1.49 (0.46).

references.relevance: Provides the Seasonal Naive method and MASE calculation approach

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 14 candidate forecasting methods need evaluation against baseline. [Gap] Need to identify which methods achieve MASE < 1.0 to outperform naive benchmark. [Decision] To screen candidate models, chose to use multiple forecasting libraries (pmdarima, fbprophet, statsmodels, tbats, forecast-tools, Rssa) to evaluate 14 methods, expecting to identify top performers for elite screening.

parameters.methods_included: Holt-Winters Exponential Smoothing, ARIMA, Harmonic regression, TBATS, SSA, Facebook Prophet, ensembles
parameters.harmonic_regression: Fourier terms selected by minimizing Akaike Information Criterion

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Stage 1 screening completed. Only Prophet (model 7) and Prophet-ARIMA ensemble (model 13) achieved MASE < 1.0 up to 84-day horizon. Prophet MASE_84 = 0.97 (0.12), ensemble MASE_84 = 0.99 (0.13).

references.relevance: Modern forecasting method designed for higher frequency data with multiple periodicity

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Two elite methods (Prophet and Prophet-ARIMA ensemble) identified from Stage 1. [Gap] Need to determine which provides better prediction interval coverage consistency. [Decision] To select final benchmark, chose to compare Prophet and ensemble at sub-region level evaluating MASE and prediction interval coverage, expecting to identify method with superior coverage consistency.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: MASE similar across all sub-regions for both methods. Ensemble prediction interval coverage more consistent than Prophet. Prophet's upper quartile coverage fails to achieve desired coverage from 21-day horizon. Ensemble selected as benchmark model.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ensemble method selected but ARIMA component needs specification. [Gap] Need to define exact ARIMA error process for regression component. [Decision] To finalize benchmark model, chose to use ARIMA errors with specific parameters (1,1,3)(1,0,1,7) for regression component, expecting to obtain reproducible model configuration.

parameters.ensemble_composition: simple average of Facebook Prophet and Regression with ARIMA errors
parameters.special_events: New Year's day included as binary intervention variable
parameters.implementation: same ARIMA model for each sub-region

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Final benchmark model configured as ensemble of Prophet + Regression with ARIMA errors (1,1,3)(1,0,1,7). Model flexible enough to add calendar events.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Benchmark model selected and configured. [Gap] Need to establish benchmark accuracy metrics in simulated forecast setting before external validation. [Decision] To establish benchmark performance, chose to use time series cross-validation on test set with 365 observations per sub-region, expecting to obtain MASE and coverage metrics for 7-84 day horizon.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Benchmark MASE: 0.68 (95% CI 0.67-0.69). 80% PI coverage: 0.847 (95% CI 0.843-0.851). 95% PI coverage: 0.965 (95% CI 0.949-0.977). 90% of mean point forecast errors between 0.49 and 0.91. Equivalent sMAPE: 4.9% (95% CI 4.7-5.1).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Benchmark established on SWASFT data. [Gap] Need to validate model generalizability across different ambulance trusts. [Decision] To externally validate benchmark, chose to apply ensemble method to 13 test sets from London, Yorkshire and Wales, expecting to confirm performance within expected benchmark ranges.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: External validation MASE: 0.73 (95% CI 0.72-0.74). 90% of validation folds achieved MASE between 0.64 and 0.83. 80% PI coverage: 0.833 (95% CI 0.828-0.838). 95% PI coverage: 0.965 (95% CI 0.963-0.967). Performance within expected benchmark ranges.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model validated externally with strong performance. [Gap] Need to make benchmark accessible for implementation by ambulance services. [Decision] To enable practical deployment, chose to release MIT licensed Python package with Docker image and cloud tutorial, expecting to facilitate adoption by NHS ambulance services.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Benchmark model released as open Python package. Docker image contains exact software, code and data. Cloud runnable tutorial available. Results implemented in South West of England ambulance service.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Time series cross-validation with external validation across multiple ambulance trusts

benchmark_MASE.interpretation: Mean Absolute Scaled Error on SWASFT test set (95% CI 0.67-0.69), indicating 32% improvement over seasonal naive baseline

external_validation_MASE.interpretation: MASE on 13 external regions (95% CI 0.72-0.74), confirming model generalizability within expected range

benchmark_80_percent_PI_coverage.interpretation: 80% prediction interval coverage on benchmark (95% CI 0.843-0.851), slightly conservative but acceptable

benchmark_95_percent_PI_coverage.interpretation: 95% prediction interval coverage on benchmark (95% CI 0.949-0.977), meeting expected coverage

external_80_percent_PI_coverage.interpretation: 80% PI coverage on external validation (95% CI 0.828-0.838), consistent with benchmark

external_95_percent_PI_coverage.interpretation: 95% PI coverage on external validation (95% CI 0.963-0.967), matching benchmark performance

forecast_horizon.interpretation: Maximum forecast horizon evaluated, covering 12 weeks for workforce planning

sMAPE.interpretation: Symmetric Mean Absolute Percentage Error over 84-day horizon (95% CI 4.7-5.1), comparable to existing studies

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. Successfully selected and externally validated a benchmark forecasting method (ensemble of Prophet + ARIMA errors) for emergency ambulance services. Model achieves MASE of 0.68 on benchmark and 0.73 on external validation over 84-day horizon, with adequate prediction interval coverage (80%: 0.847/0.833, 95%: 0.965/0.965). Results implemented in South West England ambulance service with open-source Python framework provided for adoption.
